title: Vehicle Mechanics Domain Modeling
use_for: extracting and redesigning vehicle-related mechanics in a neutral domain model
separating physical concepts, tuning parameters, telemetry, commands, and UI presentation
modeling mechanics such as suspension, tires, brakes, powertrain, setup presets, and telemetry-driven tuning
keeping vehicle mechanics independent from a named game, mod, or host implementation
do_not_use_for: general runtime architecture with no vehicle or simulation mechanics
rules: model mechanics in domain language before binding to host APIs or UI controls
separate measured telemetry, authored configuration, derived analysis, and mutation commands
make units, ranges, normalization, and sign conventions explicit
treat presets and tuning profiles as domain inputs, not UI state
record uncertainty when old behavior does not reveal a physical or product rationale
procedure: List mechanics by domain area: chassis, suspension, tires, brakes, powertrain, aero, setup, telemetry, or driver assistance as relevant.
For each area, define inputs, parameters, state, derived metrics, decisions, commands, and validation cases.
Separate physical concepts from host-specific object names and UI labels.
Define units, valid ranges, default values, and conversion boundaries.
Classify behavior as physical rule, tuning policy, compatibility behavior, UI convenience, or unknown.
Feed the model into domain pipelines, command mutation, replay validation, and UI surfaces.

primary_when: the task is primarily about vehicle or simulation mechanics domain modeling
must_not_own_when: the task is primarily host runtime lifecycle, UI layout, or non-vehicle domain logic

solves: how to describe vehicle mechanics without copying old implementation structure
how to keep telemetry, analysis, tuning, and mutation separated
how to avoid binding mechanics to one game or project
does_not_solve: accurate physics engine design by itself

core_principles: Vehicle mechanics should be modeled as measured inputs, domain state, derived analysis, and controlled outputs.
Units and ranges are part of the domain contract.
A tuning profile is domain data, not a UI artifact.
Host-specific names should be translated at boundaries.
constraints: Do not include named games, mods, local paths, or project-specific class names.
Do not treat UI slider ranges as authoritative physics ranges without validation.
Do not mix telemetry capture with tuning decisions in one owner.
Do not hide unit conversions inside unrelated domain logic.
decision_rules: If data comes from runtime measurement, model it as telemetry input.
If data configures behavior, model it as authored or user configuration.
If data is computed from telemetry, model it as derived analysis.
If behavior changes external simulation state, model it as a command.
If a value has unclear units or sign, block architectural promotion until the convention is explicit.

avoid_patterns: host object names as mechanics model
copying old physics workarounds without rationale

recipes.name: Model a vehicle mechanics subsystem
recipes.when: Use when a rewrite needs a clean domain model for one vehicle-related capability.
steps: Name the mechanic in neutral domain language.
List telemetry inputs, configuration inputs, derived metrics, and command outputs.
Define units, ranges, and conversion boundaries.
Separate physical rules from tuning policies.
Create replay or scenario validation cases for representative behavior.

pitfalls: Preserving old variable names as domain truth.
Letting UI controls define the mechanics model.
Skipping unit and sign convention documentation.
Mixing compatibility hacks with physical rules.
review_checklist: Are mechanics described without project-specific terms?
Are telemetry, configuration, analysis, and commands separated?
Are tuning profiles treated as domain data?

references.title: Contracts
references.interpretation: Use for mechanics parameter, telemetry, and command contracts.
references.why: Vehicle mechanics need explicit domain contracts.

references.title: Stage Boundaries
references.interpretation: Use to separate telemetry, analysis, decisions, and mutation stages.
references.why: Simulation mechanics often fit pipeline stage boundaries.
